scenario_title: Add and delete the same RRset in one UPDATE
test_rationale: This test creates a single syntactically valid UPDATE that first adds a TXT RRset and then deletes that same RRset. The protocol does not mandate whether the server must apply operations in order, collapse them as a set, or reject the conflict, so BIND and Knot may differ either in the UPDATE reply code or in the resulting zone state. A follow-up QUERY on the touched name makes the outcome observable in a binary way.

group_1.behavior: Applies the UPDATE operations sequentially or treats the delete as removing the just-added RRset, resulting in no TXT RRset after a successful UPDATE.

group_2.behavior: Rejects the conflicting in-message add/delete for the same RRset as a semantic error, or preserves the TXT RRset if it interprets the later delete as ineffective.
group_2.post_state: TXT RRset present at a.example.campus.edu. or unchanged failure state

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 defines the UPDATE message format and operation types, but it does not specify a required evaluation order or conflict-resolution rule when an add_rr and delete_rrset target the same owner/type within one Update Section. RFC 2136 §7.2 also notes that data-present/absent conditions belong in prerequisites, not as update-section error conditions, leaving this same-message contradiction underspecified.
